name: Kakavia
recipeIngredient: Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the fish for poaching: Select bass, cod, hake, haddock, halibut, trout, pollack, snapper, rockfish, or whiting.
2. In a soup pot with a wide bottom, sauté the onions in olive oil until soft, without browning them.
3. Add the tomatoes, fennel, herbs, white wine, and water. Bring to a boil.
4. Season with salt and pepper to taste and simmer for 45 minutes.
5. Strain the stock into a large bowl through a sieve, squeezing all the pulp from the vegetables before discarding the fibers.
6. Return the strained stock to the pot and bring to a boil.
7. Meanwhile, lightly season the fish and let stand for 10 minutes. Rinse with water and lower into the soup, adding water only if needed to cover the fish.
8. Lower the heat and simmer for 5 minutes. Add the lobster (if using) and simmer for an additional 5 minutes.
9. Add the shrimp and scallops or mussels (if using) and simmer for another 10 minutes. Adjust seasonings as needed.
10. Arrange toast slices in large soup plates. Serve the varied fishes and broth in each bowl, hot, with croutons on a separate plate.
NOTE: Kakavia can also be made by cooking 2 to 3 fish with the vegetables, straining them to make a thicker stock, and continuing to cook the remaining seafood as described above.
